install.macos_download: Open https://opencode.ai/download in the browser, click the macOS download (.dmg, Apple Silicon or Intel). Saves to ~/Downloads by default.
install.windows_download: Open https://opencode.ai/download in the browser, click the Windows (x64) download — a one-click NSIS .exe installer. Saves to %USERPROFILE%\Downloads by default.
install.linux_download: Open https://opencode.ai/download in the browser, download the .deb / .rpm / AppImage and install it.
install.note: GUI desktop application (Electron). It shares config under ~/.config/opencode with the OpenCode CLI, so a model configured for one applies to both. Always tell the user the download location and ask them to run the installer themselves.

install_flow.summary: Download installer -> user double-clicks -> installer finishes (Windows is a one-click NSIS installer; macOS/Linux follow the wizard or drag-to-Applications)
agent_steps: macOS: prefer `brew install --cask opencode-desktop` when Homebrew is available; otherwise download the .dmg from https://opencode.ai/download and open it.
Windows / Linux: download the installer from https://opencode.ai/download to the Downloads folder.
After the download finishes, tell the user the EXACT file path.
Open the installer (`Start-Process` on Windows, `open` on macOS).
Tell the user to follow the installer to finish; the Windows NSIS build installs in one click with no directory prompt.
Do NOT try to automate the GUI wizard.
tell_user: That OpenCode Desktop shares model config with the OpenCode CLI

identity_guard.description: OpenCode Desktop is the GUI app for OpenCode (the open-source AI coding agent by Anomaly). It is DIFFERENT from the OpenCode CLI (npm `opencode-ai`). Both can coexist and share config under ~/.config/opencode, so a model set for one applies to both.
